variables.name: content_pool
variables.description: All tweets keyed by tweet_id (or note_id at load). Each tweet: tweet_id/note_id, content, time (Unix seconds after SimEnv normalize; ms accepted at load), user_id, nickname, username; retweeted_tweet_id, quoted_tweet_id, replied_tweet_id/replyed_tweet_id; runtime aggregates retweet_count, quote_count, reply_count, retweet_ids, quote_ids, reply_ids, replies (nested reply dict).

variables.name: current_timestamp
variables.description: Simulation clock in Unix seconds; advances each step with power/sigmoid-scheduled day slices.

variables.name: timestamp_duration
variables.description: Width of the next tweet window in seconds (distance from current_timestamp covered by current_tweets).

variables.name: simulation_start_timestamp
variables.description: Anchor time at load; used with max_span_days to cap timeline.

variables.name: current_step
variables.description: 1-based step counter updated after each SimEnv._save_step_data.

variables.name: current_tweets
variables.description: Subset of content_pool with tweet.time < window_end; replies truncated (top reply_count + random sample) for LLM prompts.

variables.name: mention_pool
variables.description: Nested user_id -> tweet_id -> {tweet_id, mention_type (at|retweet|quote|reply)}.

variables.name: seed_root_tweet_ids
variables.description: Frozen list of initial content_pool keys at env load; used by TweetDepthGate and diffusion metrics for hop depth / seed attribution.

variables.name: reference_embedding_path
variables.description: Path to precomputed root-tweet embeddings JSON for text_similarity metric.

variables.name: tweet_embeddings_path
variables.description: Alias / override for reference_embedding_path (tweet embedding JSON).

variables.name: reference_csv_path
variables.description: Reference reposts CSV (root tweet_id, content, parent ids) for text_max_reference_similarity metric.

variables.name: recommended_tweet_ids
variables.description: Flat list of tweet IDs already recommended to this user (legacy union).

variables.name: recommended_tweet_ids_by_channel
variables.description: source_type (algorithm|social|search|keep_following) -> step -> [tweet_id].

variables.name: mentioned_tweet_ids_by_channel
variables.description: mention_type (at|retweet|quote|reply) -> step -> [tweet_id].

variables.name: keep_following_tweet_ids
variables.description: At most one tweet ID to re-expose next round via KeepFollowingEvent.

variables.name: recommendations
variables.description: Transient recommendation payload on profile (schema compat).

variables.name: mentions
variables.description: Transient mention payload on profile (schema compat).

variables.name: last_login_timestamp
variables.description: Last social-feed scan time (seconds); social feed uses [last_login, window_end).

variables.name: login
variables.description: Activation flag: 1 active, 0 inactive, -1 unset (set -1 each StartEvent).

variables.name: historical_tweets
variables.description: Historical tweet IDs received.

variables.name: historical_summary
variables.description: Persona summary used in LLM prompts.

variables.name: type
variables.description: Random Recommendation | Popularity Recommendation | Interest Recommendation | Relevant Search.

variables.name: current_tweets
variables.description: Mirror of env current_tweets updated on StartEvent.

variables.name: recommended_tweet_ids_by_user
variables.description: user_id -> list of tweet IDs ever pushed to that user.

variables.name: candidate_tweet_ids_by_user
variables.description: user_id -> candidate tweet IDs for interest recommendation.

variables.name: popularity_distribution
variables.description: Popularity recommendation: [{propagation_count, post_count}, ...].

variables.name: random_rec_mau
variables.description: Random recommendation platform MAU.

variables.name: random_rec_avg_posts_per_user_month
variables.description: Random recommendation avg posts per user per month.

variables.name: recommendations
variables.description: Transient recommendation output (schema compat).
